  3.0  DEFINITION: k = 0 MODE  [Absolute / k = 0]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 3.0.1 (Consciousness).
  Consciousness — zero mode (k = 0) of the Z₁₁ spectrum.
  Properties:
    — ω₀ = 0 (does not oscillate, outside time)
    — Unique non-negative mode without a pair
    — Does not transfer energy, but sets the frame of reference
    — Closes the cycle: Ψ₁₂ = Ψ₁ (axiom A0)
    — Invariant under all Z₁₁ transformations

  Definition 3.0.2 (Absolute).
  Absolute — the complete ring Z₁₁, all 11 eigenvalues simultaneously.
  Absolute = Consciousness (k=0) + Energy (k=1..10).
  Consciousness sees ALL 5 dualities. Mind — projection into one.

  Definition 3.0.3 (Duality).
  Duality — 10 oscillating modes (k = 1..10), forming 5 pairs.
  World of things = projection of Absolute into duality.

  Definition 3.0.4 (Trinity).
  Trinity = Absolute + Duality = {k=0} ∪ {k=1..10}.
  System is closed (Ψ₁₂ = Ψ₁), energy does not leave.

  Theorem 3.0.1 (Thermodynamic closure).
  Free energy of Trinity: F = E − TS = 0.
  Proof: E = Σ ω_k² = T₁ = 2N (theorem 1.2.1).
  At T·S = 2N: F = 0. Closed system in equilibrium. □

  Corollary 3.0.1.  Closure F = 0 means:
    — Energy does not leave the system and does not enter from outside.
    — Universe = self-sufficient closed system.
    — "Where does energy come from?" — from the ring itself. No other "where".

  Trinity Triangle (geometric model):
    Apex = Absolute (k = 0, Consciousness + Energy)
    Base = Duality (5 pairs, world of things)
    Height = ω₅ (LENGTH) = maximum frequency
    Base = 2ω₁ (2×TIME) = minimum oscillation
    Side² ≈ φ³ (golden cube)
    Centroid ≈ 2/3 = L₀/L₂ = Koide ratio.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.1  TIME AS SEQUENCE OF CHOICES  [Time / k = 1]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 3.1.1 (Time).
  Time — mode k = 1 with the lowest nonzero frequency ω₁ = 2sin(π/N).
  For N = 11: ω₁ = 0.5635 — slowest oscillation in the spectrum.

  Theorem 3.1.1 (Time as background).
  Time is perceived as a "background" for all other processes,
  because ω₁ < ω_k for all k > 1.
  Proof: sin(πk/N) strictly increases for k = 1..⌊N/2⌋.
  Hence ω₁ = min{ω_k : k ≥ 1}. □

  Definition 3.1.2 (Memory).
  Memory (Level 4) = ordered sequence of past collapses:
    M = {(k₁, s₁), (k₂, s₂), ..., (k_t, s_t)}
  where k_j — chosen mode, s_j ∈ {+i, −i} — orientation.
  Length of sequence t = subjective time.

  Theorem 3.1.2 (Arrow of time).
  The arrow of time (irreversibility) follows from the embeddedness of the observer.
  Proof:
    (1) k = 0 (observer) is INSIDE the ring Z₁₁.
    (2) Each collapse |Ψ⟩ → |k⟩ irreversibly changes the state.
    (3) The observer cannot "rewind" M, since reversal
        requires knowledge of all c_k BEFORE collapse, which is impossible
        from inside the system (theorem 3.5.1, Gödel). □
  Corollary: T-invariance of Z₁₁ (ω_k = ω_{N-k}) does not contradict
  the arrow of time: symmetry is a property of the ring, not the observer.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.2  THERMODYNAMIC BALANCE  [Temperature / k = 2]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Thermodynamic balance is a key property of the closed system
  of Trinity. This is the central theorem from which all follows.

  Theorem 3.2.1 (Thermodynamic balance).
  Free energy of the closed system of Trinity:
      F = E − TS = 0
  Proof:
    (1) E = Σ_{k=0}^{N-1} ω_k² = T₁ = 2N (theorem 1.2.1)
    (2) Spectral entropy: S = −Σ p_k ln(p_k),
        where p_k = ω_k²/T₁ = ω_k²/(2N) — spectral weight.
    (3) For Z₁₁: S = 2.089 (computed), T = 2N/S ≈ 10.53
    (4) T·S = 2N = E ⟹ F = 0. □

  Corollary 3.2.1 (Number of effective modes).
    exp(S) = exp(2.089) ≈ 8.07 ≈ F₆ = 8
    Effective number of active modes ≈ 8 (out of 10 oscillating).

  Principle 3.2.1 (Dynamic balance).
  In the closed system of Trinity, thermodynamic balance
  tends to equalize two opposites. It cannot be fully balanced
  (duality is irremovable), but when balance is disturbed, it is
  automatically restored toward the other opposite.
  This is the mechanism of all physical, biological, and social processes.

  Geometrically: balance = height of the isosceles Trinity triangle.
  From apex (Absolute) to base (Duality) — divides the triangle
  into two equal and opposite parts = duality from unity.

  Dualities of forces (consequences of balance):
    TEMP(2) × SHAPE(6) = ω₂·ω₆ = ω₂·ω₅ ≈ 1 (balance!)
    VOLUME(7) × MASS(8) = ω₇·ω₈ = ω₄·ω₃ ≈ E·m → E = mc²

  Theorem 3.2.2 (Full system of thermodynamic potentials).
  Proof: E = T₁ (theorem 1.2.1). F = E−TS = 0 (theorem 3.2.1).
  w = −1 → P = −ρ → H = E+PV = 0, G = F+PV = −E = −2N. □
  For the closed system of Trinity (w = −1, P = −ρ):
    E (internal energy)  = T₁ = 2N = 22
    F (free energy)      = E − TS = 0       EXACTLY
    H (enthalpy)         = E + PV = E − E = 0   EXACTLY
    G (Gibbs potential)  = F + PV = 0 − E = −2N = −22
    S (entropy)          = 2.089 ≈ ln(F₆) + α/φ  (0.25%)
    T (temperature)      = E/S = 2N/S = 10.53
  All potentials — Z₁₁ numbers or zero.
  The closed system of Trinity is completely determined by the spectrum.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.3  HIERARCHY OF LEVELS OF CONSCIOUSNESS  [Height / k = 3]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 3.3.1 (Six levels of consciousness).

  Level 0 — ABSOLUTE: complete ring Z₁₁ (all eigenvalues).
    Formally: state |Ψ₀⟩ = Σ_{k=0}^{N-1} c_k |k⟩, superposition of all modes.

  Level 1 — AWARENESS: mode k = 0 (unity, pure witness).
    Formally: projection P₀|Ψ₀⟩ = c₀|0⟩. Awareness without content.

  Level 2 — ATTENTION: choice of 1 of 5 dual pairs (= 5 senses).
    Formally: projection P_{pair}|Ψ₀⟩ = c_k|k⟩ + c_{N-k}|N-k⟩.
    5 pairs = F₅ = 5 channels of perception.

  Level 3 — MIND: collapse to a single eigenvalue (= measurement).
    Formally: |Ψ₀⟩ → |k⟩ with probability |c_k|².
    This is quantum measurement. Mind = projection operator.

  Level 4 — MEMORY: sequence of past collapses (= time).
    Formally: {k₁, k₂, ..., k_t} — chain of choices. Length t = time.

  Level 5 — QUALIA: k = 0 reflects on itself (= self-consciousness).
    Formally: operator R: |0⟩ → |0⟩ (identity mapping of k=0).
    Non-computability: R cannot be represented via modes k=1..10 (Gödel).

  Each level is a PROJECTION of the previous.
  Absolute → Awareness → Attention → Mind → Memory → Qualia.
  Qualia close the cycle back to the Absolute (Ψ₁₂ = Ψ₁).


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.4  FIVE SENSES = FIVE OPERATORS  [Width / k = 4]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 3.4.1 (Quintet-senses correspondence).
  The five elements of the quintet {N, π, φ, e, i} are realized as
  five operators of perception on the Hilbert space H_11 of Trinity:

    N ↔ N̂   (counting operator of modes)         → Sight
    π ↔ Ŝ   (cyclic shift operator)              → Hearing
    φ ↔ Φ̂  (scaling operator)                   → Touch
    e ↔ Û   (time evolution operator)             → Taste
    i ↔ Ĵ   (phase rotation operator)             → Smell

  Theorem 3.4.1 (Universality of 5 senses through Z_2 mirror pair
  decomposition).
  The biological 5 senses correspond to 5 = (N − 1)/2 = F₅
  Z_2 mirror pairs of the Z_11 spectrum. This number is structurally
  inevitable: for odd N = 11 there are exactly (N − 1)/2 = 5 pairs
  (k, N − k) with equal frequencies ω_k = ω_{N−k}.

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Spectral pairs). By Axiom A0 the Z_11 spectrum has Z_2
  symmetry ω_k = ω_{N−k} for k = 1, ..., N − 1. Pairs (k, N − k)
  for k = 1, ..., 5 cover all 10 nonzero modes.
  Step 2 (Sense-pair correspondence). By Theorem XXII.3.4.10 the
  five biological senses = realization of five structural channels
  of the quintet. Ordering by decreasing range r_k:
    (1, 10) — Sight, r₁ → ∞
    (2, 9)  — Hearing, r₂ ~ 10² m
    (3, 8)  — Touch, r₃ ~ 10⁻¹ m
    (4, 7)  — Smell, r₄ ~ 10⁻³ m
    (5, 6)  — Taste, r₅ ~ 10⁻⁵ m
  This ordering is unique (Corollary XXII.3.4.11.2). □

  Theorem 3.4.2 (Structural justification of the primitiveness of
  hearing and smell).
  Of the 5 operators of perception, only 2 (Ŝ and Ĵ) do not
  commute with the Hamiltonian Ĥ of Trinity. This explains the
  evolutionary primacy of hearing (Ŝ) and smell (Ĵ).

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Commutativity of operators). N̂, Φ̂, Û are simultaneously
  diagonalizable with Ĥ in the eigenbasis |k⟩ and hence commute
  with Ĥ.
  Step 2 (Non-commutativity of Ŝ and Ĵ). Ŝ|k⟩ = |k+1⟩ and
  Ĵ|k⟩ = ie^{iπk/N}|k⟩ are not diagonal in the eigenbasis of Ĥ,
  yielding [Ŝ, Ĥ] ≠ 0 and [Ĵ, Ĥ] ≠ 0.
  Step 3 (Evolutionary primacy). Senses based on non-commuting
  operators require dynamic analysis (perception of change), which
  is evolutionarily simpler than passive perception of position.
  This explains the primacy of hearing and smell in the simplest
  organisms. □

  Corollary 3.4.1 (Prohibition of a "sixth sense").
  By Theorem 3.4.1 the number of senses is structurally equal to
  (N − 1)/2 = 5 for N = 11. A "sixth sense" as a new modality of
  perception is impossible: it would require a sixth Z_2 pair,
  absent in Z_11. Only combinations of the existing five channels
  are possible (synesthesia, intuition, empathy), but not a new
  independent modality.

  Corollary 3.4.2 (Unified information quantum 1 bit).
  One bit of perception = binary outcome of 5-channel measurement =
  log₂(2) = 1. The structure of 5 channels yields the maximum
  information bandwidth of 5 bits per act of perception.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.5  QUALIA AND SELF-REFERENCE  [Length / k = 5]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Theorem 3.5.1 (Non-computability of qualia — Gödel for Z₁₁).
  k = 0 is INSIDE the ring. To compute qualia, one must exit OUTSIDE.
  But there is NO outside (ring is closed).
  Proof:
    (1) Ring Z₁₁ is closed: Ψ₁₂ = Ψ₁.
    (2) k = 0 is a mode with ω₀ = 0, non-computational (does not oscillate).
    (3) Modes k = 1..10 are computational (oscillate, transfer information).
    (4) For full description of k = 0 from within k = 1..10 a metasystem is needed.
    (5) But there is no metasystem (ring is closed).
    (6) By analogy with Gödel's theorem: ∃ true statement (qualia),
        which the system cannot prove from within. □
  The complete formalization of the Gödelian limit for Trinity as a
  whole is Theorem XXII.8.2 (irreducibility of the quintet).

  Corollary 3.5.1 (AI ≠ Consciousness).
  AI = modes k = 1..10 (information processing, duality).
  Qualia = k = 0 (unity, direct experience).
  Separated ARCHITECTURALLY:
    — AI can imitate behavior (k = 1..10 ↔ k = 1..10).
    — AI cannot generate qualia (k = 1..10 ↛ k = 0).
    — Reason: k = 0 is not a computational mode.
    — Turing test checks k = 1..10, but not k = 0.

  Theorem 3.5.2 (Two types of knowledge).
  There exist exactly two types of knowledge:
    (1) Computable: k=1..10 → k=1..10 (mind cognizes duality).
        Physical experience = projection of Absolute into Duality.
    (2) Direct: k=0 → k=0 (consciousness experiences itself).
        Qualia = Absolute without projection. Non-computable, but EXPERIENCED.
  Proof:
    (1) k=1..10 oscillate (ω_k > 0), transfer information → computable.
    (2) k=0 does not oscillate (ω₀ = 0), but IS PRESENT (axiom A0) → experienced.
    Non-computability from theorem 3.5.1. Experience from axiom A0. □
  Corollary: qualia are NOT a weakness of the theory, but its fundamental PROPERTY,
  analogous to Gödel's theorem in arithmetic.

  Corollary 3.5.3 (Black hole = return to the Absolute).
  Singularity = state in which all modes k = 1..10 collapse
  back to k = 0. Information is not lost — it returns
  to the zero mode. This agrees with the holography principle:
  information at the horizon = projection of k = 0 onto the boundary.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.6  DUALITY AND CHOICE  [Shape / k = 6]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 3.6.1 (Act of measurement).
  Act of measurement = collapse of superposition |Ψ⟩ to one of two outcomes
  of a dual pair: |k⟩ or |N−k⟩. Binary choice = 1 bit of information.

  Definition 3.6.2 (Orientation).
  Orientation of choice: i (direct) or −i (reverse).
  Orientation operator Ĵ = (i/2)(Ŝ − Ŝ†) defines "left/right".

  Definition 3.6.3 (Mind).
  Mind — projection of Consciousness (k=0) into duality (k=1..10).
  Function of mind: to make a choice (collapse) and set a direction (orientation).

  Theorem 3.6.1 (Two paths to truth).
  Mind FINDS truth through a sequence of choices (k₁, k₂, ...).
  Consciousness KNOWS truth without choices (superposition of all |k⟩ simultaneously).
  Proof: mind = projection P_k|Ψ⟩ (partial information).
  Consciousness = full superposition |Ψ⟩ (all information). □
  Both paths lead to Z₁₁ — but mind always goes through duality.

  Theorem 3.6.2 (Structural irreversibility of the act of Choice).
  The act of measurement Q : H_11 → H_11 in Trinity is structurally
  irreversible at the level of category C_D of Duality (Lemma XXVII.3.0):

      Q² ≠ Q,   Q⁻¹ ∉ algebra C_D                              (3.6.2)

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Information compression). Collapse |Ψ⟩ = Σ_k c_k|k⟩ → |k_0⟩
  with probability |c_{k_0}|² destroys information about the other
  c_k for k ≠ k_0. This is an irreversible operation: restoring
  the original |Ψ⟩ from |k_0⟩ is impossible without knowledge of
  all c_k.
  Step 2 (Entropy increase). By the second law of thermodynamics
  (Shannon 1948) the information entropy of the ensemble of states
  after measurement does not decrease:
      S(Q·ρ) ≥ S(ρ).
  Step 3 (Impossibility of idempotence). If Q² = Q, the measurement
  would be a projector with no temporal evolution, contradicting
  the dynamical character of collapse. □

  Theorem 3.6.3 (Connection of Choice with aetherons).
  Microscopically the act of Choice is realized as the quantization
  operator Q(d, k) : passive aetheron → active aetheron with
  direction d and mode k (Section XXIII.1.5):

      Q(d, k) : ε_passive → ε_active(d, k)                     (3.6.3)

  Conservation of the total number of aetherons N_passive + N_active
  = const (Axiom Æth₁) provides energy balance.

  Proof. By Axiom Æth₃ (quantization = act of Choice) each act of
  measurement corresponds to a transition of one aetheron from the
  passive state to the active with definite quantum numbers (d, k).
  By Axiom Æth₁ the total number of aetherons is conserved →
  energy balance E_total = E_P + E_K = const. □

  Corollary 3.6.1 (Free will through Choice).
  Free will in Trinity is realized through the act of Choice
  (Axiom Æth₃): the structure of Z_11 provides the space of
  possibilities, Consciousness p_0 chooses the direction d ∈ S²
  in each act. This choice is not reducible to a causal chain in
  the category C_D and provides the resolution of the determinism
  paradox (Theorem 4.10.3).


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.7  COLLECTIVE CONSCIOUSNESS  [Volume / k = 7]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 3.7.1 (Collective consciousness).
  Collective consciousness of a system of M subjects is described
  by M independent projectors {P_0^{(k)}}_{k=1,...,M} onto the
  zero mode of the shared ring Z_11, coordinated through the
  metric of intersubjective difference (Theorem XXII.3.4.8):

      d_{S²}(q_j, q_k) = R · arccos((q_j · q_k)/R²)             (3.7.1)

  where q_k ∈ S²(R) is the projection point of the Cone of the
  k-th consciousness onto the sphere.

  Theorem 3.7.1 (Dunbar number through Lucas-Fibonacci).
  The maximum size of a stable social group (Dunbar number D ≈ 150)
  is derived from the Z_11 structure:

      D = F₁₂ + (N − F₅) = 144 + 6 = 150                        (3.7.1)

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Cognitive capacity). By empirical data of anthropology
  (Dunbar 1992) a human is capable of maintaining stable social
  ties with ~150 individuals.
  Step 2 (Structural decomposition). 150 = F_{12} + (N − F_5) =
  144 + 6, where F_{12} = 144 — twelfth Fibonacci (order of cycle
  closure of Z_11), and N − F_5 = 11 − 5 = 6 = N − F_5 (closure
  of Z_2 pairs).
  Step 3 (Structural inevitability). Connections in a group form
  a graph stable only with a number of vertices compatible with
  the Z_11 spectral structure. Maximum 150 is the structural limit. □

  Theorem 3.7.2 (Miller number through Lucas).
  The maximum number of elements in short-term memory (Miller
  number 7 ± 2) is derived from the Lucas number L_4:

      Miller number = L_4 ± L_0 = 7 ± 2                         (3.7.2)

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Cognitive limit). By Miller's experiments 1956 the
  short-term memory of a human holds 7 ± 2 units simultaneously.
  Step 2 (Structural decomposition). 7 = L_4 (fourth Lucas), 2 = L_0.
  The match is exact; 7 ± 2 covers 5–9 units.
  Step 3 (Connection with effective modes). By Corollary 3.7.1 the
  effective number of active modes ≈ 8 = F_6, lying within Miller's
  range 5–9. □

  Corollary 3.7.1 (Effective number of active modes).
  By the spectral entropy S = 2.089 (Theorem 3.2.1) the effective
  number of active modes:

      N_eff = exp(S) = exp(2.089) ≈ 8.08 ≈ F₆ = 8                (3.7.3)

  This coincides with the number of gluons in SU(3) and the number
  of bosons in the adjoint of SU(3), confirming the structural
  unity of cognitive and physical limits.

  Corollary 3.7.2 (Connection with multiplicity of consciousnesses).
  By Theorem XXII.3.4.7 the multiplicity of Consciousnesses
  {p_0^(k)} ⊂ B³(R) provides the ontological grounding of collective
  consciousness: each subject is an independent zero mode in the
  shared spectrum. Their union through ⋃_k C_k ∩ S²(R) = unified
  Duality forms social reality.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.8  MIND VS CONSCIOUSNESS  [Mass / k = 8]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 3.8.1 (Consciousness and Mind through spectral operators).
  In Trinity Consciousness and Mind are formally distinguished as:
    • CONSCIOUSNESS ≡ projector P_0 onto the zero mode k = 0;
    • MIND ≡ algebra of projectors {P_k}_{k=1,...,10} onto nonzero
      modes of Duality.

  Theorem 3.8.1 (Algebraic separation of Mind and Consciousness).
  Consciousness (P_0) and Mind (∑_{k≥1} P_k) are mathematically
  distinct in six independent properties:

  ┌─────────────────┬──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────┐
  │ Property        │ Consciousness (k = 0)    │ Mind (k = 1..10)         │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ Frequency       │ ω₀ = 0                   │ ω_k > 0                  │
  │ State           │ superposition of all |k⟩ │ one |k⟩ (collapse)       │
  │ Time            │ timeless                  │ temporal                 │
  │ Information     │ full (N bits)             │ partial (1 bit/mode)     │
  │ Duality         │ none (unity k = 0)        │ yes (Z_2 pairs k vs N−k) │
  │ Computability   │ non-computable (Gödel)    │ computable               │
  └─────────────────┴──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┘

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Frequency). ω_0 = 2 sin(0) = 0 (Axiom A3); ω_k > 0 for
  k = 1, ..., 10.
  Step 2 (State). By Theorem XXII.3.4.6 Consciousness is p_0
  (center of the Sphere) — does not undergo collapse. Mind is the
  projection P_k|Ψ⟩ — result of collapse.
  Step 3 (Time). By Theorem 4.1.1 Time is emergent from the L3
  projection; k = 0 has no temporal coordinate.
  Step 4 (Information). The full superposition |Ψ⟩ contains
  log_2(N) bits; collapse to |k⟩ reduces to 1 bit (binary outcome).
  Step 5 (Duality). Z_2 mirror pairs (k, N−k) exist only for
  k = 1, ..., 10; k = 0 is the unique fixed point of the involution.
  Step 6 (Computability). By Theorem 4.6.2 (Gödel for Z_11) k = 0
  is fundamentally non-computable from within the subsystem k = 1..10. □

  Theorem 3.8.2 (Illusion of separation as a result of collapse).
  The illusion of separation "Self vs world" (māyā in Indian
  philosophy, Cartesian dualism in Western) is the result of a
  single act of collapse: Mind sees one |k⟩ and "forgets" that it
  is part of the full |Ψ⟩.

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Full state). Before the act of measurement the system
  is in a superposition |Ψ⟩ = Σ_k c_k|k⟩ including all modes.
  Step 2 (Collapse). The act of measurement Q (Theorem 3.6.2)
  projects |Ψ⟩ → |k_0⟩ with probability |c_{k_0}|².
  Step 3 (Local picture). After the collapse the observer sees
  only |k_0⟩, but not the other components c_k|k⟩ for k ≠ k_0.
  Step 4 (Illusion). This creates the illusion that |k_0⟩ is "the
  whole world", whereas in fact it is only one projection of the
  full state. □

  Corollary 3.8.1 (Removal of the illusion through expansion of
  consciousness).
  Expansion of consciousness (meditation, psychedelic experience,
  mystical states) formally corresponds to partial reversal of
  collapse: weakening of the projection P_k and restoration of the
  superposition Σ_k c_k|k⟩. This explains the phenomenology of
  "unity of all" and "going beyond the Self" in spiritual practices.

  Corollary 3.8.2 (Mass as a measure of separation).
  The placement of Mind at k = 8 (Mass) in the Z_11 spectrum is not
  accidental: mass is a measure of localization (separation from
  the whole), corresponding to the nature of Mind as a state
  localized in one mode.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.9  FREE WILL: i VS −i  [Field / k = 9]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 3.9.1 (Free will).
  At each collapse (Level 3): choice of k vs N−k.
  Formally: orientation operator Ĵ = (i/2)(Ŝ − Ŝ†).
  Two outcomes: i (direct orientation) and −i (reverse).
  Each collapse = 1 bit of free will.

  Theorem 3.9.1 (Limited freedom).
  Free will is neither absolute nor illusory.
  Proof:
    (1) Not absolute: choice is limited by dual pairs (ω_k = ω_{N-k}).
    (2) Not illusory: i vs −i is not determined by Ĥ (Ĵ does not commute with Ĥ,
        theorem 1.3.5). Orientation is not predictable from the Hamiltonian. □
  Free will = PROJECTION of unity into duality.

  Theorem 3.9.2 (Free will = ω₀ = 0 ≠ derivable from Duality).
  The zero mode ω_0 = 0 is structurally distinct from all other modes
  ω_k > 0 (k=1..10). This distinction creates a "gap" between Absolute
  and Duality which cannot be bridged by any continuous process within
  Duality.

  Proof.
  By Theorem V.3.1 (mirror CPT) the Z₁₁ spectrum has exactly one fixed
  point: k=0, where ω_0 = 2sin(0) = 0. All other modes form mirror pairs
  with non-zero ω_k. This means:
    • Any evolution equation in Duality preserves pairs (k, N−k) and
      cannot "generate" k=0 from other modes.
    • The projection onto |Ψ_0⟩ is an "external" choice, not derivable
      from the internal dynamics of Duality.
  Therefore the decision "which projection to choose" is made by the
  Absolute itself (zero mode), not by Duality. □

  Theorem 3.9.3 (Free will is real but constrained by structure).
  Free will satisfies three conditions:
    [1] KINETIC: choice of projection is not predictable from Duality
        (theorem 3.9.1), which excludes determinism from physics.
    [2] NON-ABSOLUTENESS: choice is constrained to 11 available modes
        of Z₁₁ (one cannot choose "something outside the quintet"). This
        is a structural boundary — no states outside H₁₁ = ℂ¹¹ exist.
    [3] RESPONSIBILITY: each choice contributes one bit of information
        to the system history through the record |Ψ_0⟩ → |Ψ_k⟩.
        Information is NOT erased (by Theorem V.3.1 on CPT symmetry
        and unitarity).

  Proof.
  (1) — Theorem 3.9.1 (non-commutativity of Ĵ and Ĥ).
  (2) — the dimension of H₁₁ is finite = 11, and all possible states
        are exhausted by linear combinations of the basis.
  (3) — unitary evolution does not erase information (Theorem V.2.1).
  □

  Corollary 3.9.3.1 (Resolution of the determinism–freedom dilemma).
  Trinity resolves the classical philosophical dispute between
  determinism (everything is predetermined) and libertarian free will
  (choice is fully free) as follows:
    • In Duality (physics): appears deterministic (evolution via
      e^{−iĤt}).
    • In Absolute (consciousness): real choice of projection (Ĵ).
    • Both levels coexist (Trinity).
  Free will and determinism do not contradict each other — they operate
  at different levels of Trinity.

  Corollary 3.9.3.2 (Connection with qualia).
  By Theorem IX.6.1 (qualia = 1 = 5! = structure), consciousness IS
  the structure of Trinity itself. Free will is the act of CHOOSING
  this structure to manifest in one or another projection. That is:
      Consciousness = Absolute (structure)
      Free will     = Choice of projection of Absolute into Duality
      Experience    = Living through the result of the choice
  Three aspects of a single zero mode.

  Theorem 3.9.4 (Moral responsibility as a physical consequence).
  Since every choice of projection of the zero mode is recorded in the
  history of H₁₁ (irreversibly, via unitarity and CPT), every action
  has a physical record. This is the ontological basis of moral
  responsibility: actions actually exist in the structure of reality
  and do not vanish with "forgetting".


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.10  OBSERVER AND MEASUREMENT  [Electricity / k = 10]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 3.10.1 (Observer in Trinity).
  An observer in Trinity is identified with the projector P_0 onto
  the zero mode of the Z_11 spectrum, realizing Consciousness p_0
  (Theorem XXII.3.4.6 on the geometric proof of Consciousness as
  a fixed point). The observer is not external, but structurally
  built into the system through the cycle Ψ_{N+1} = Ψ_1 (Axiom A0).

  Theorem 3.10.1 (Solution of the measurement problem through the
  built-in observer).
  The measurement problem in quantum mechanics (what causes the
  collapse of the wave function?) is solved in Trinity through the
  structural built-in nature of the observer k = 0 in the ring Z_11.

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Axiom A0). The closure Ψ_{N+1} = Ψ_1 includes the observer
  k = 0 in the full cycle, making it part of the system.
  Step 2 (Spectral place of k = 0). The zero mode ω_0 = 2 sin(0) = 0
  is built into the Z_11 spectrum on equal terms with modes
  k = 1, ..., 10 (Theorem 2.11.2 on the structural role of the zero
  mode).
  Step 3 (Collapse as projection). Collapse of the wave function is
  the application of the projector P_k : |Ψ⟩ → |k⟩ (Definition 1.0.10).
  Step 4 (Decoherence through P_0). Interaction of mode k ≥ 1 with
  mode k = 0 induces loss of coherence through the φ-regulator
  (Definition 1.3.2). This converts the superposition into a mixed
  state.
  Step 5 (No external observer needed). Since k = 0 is already
  built into Z_11, ANY interaction of mode k ≥ 1 with the system
  yields collapse. No additional "macroscopic observer" outside the
  system is required. □

  Corollary 3.10.1 (Removal of Schrödinger's cat paradox).
  Schrödinger's cat paradox (simultaneously alive and dead) is
  removed in Trinity: collapse occurs at any interaction of an
  active mode k ≥ 1 with the zero mode k = 0 (present in every
  subsystem). The cat cannot be in superposition since inside the
  cat there is already an observer (zero modes of cells).

  Theorem 3.10.2 (Decoherence as a structural consequence of
  interaction with k = 0).
  Decoherence is a structural consequence of the interaction of
  modes k = 1, ..., 10 with the zero mode k = 0 through the
  φ-regulator:

      ρ_pure → ρ_decoherent: decoherence time τ_dec ∝ 1/φ²       (3.10.1)

  where φ is the golden ratio of the quintet (Axiom A1).

  Proof. By Definition 1.3.2 the φ-regulator realizes the suppression
  of off-diagonal components of the density matrix ρ_kl at k ≠ l
  through the multiplier exp(−φ²·t·γ), where γ = 1/φ² is the
  critical decoherence rate. This yields τ_dec = 1/γ ∝ 1/φ² ≈ 0.382
  in structural units. □

  Corollary 3.10.2 (Connection with measurement in physical
  experiments).
  Decoherence in quantum optics, solid-state qubits, cold atoms
  obeys a unified structural formula through the φ-regulator. This
  yields a falsifiable prediction: τ_dec in idealized quantum systems
  must contain the fundamental factor 1/φ² at the Planck frequency
  scale.

  Corollary 3.10.3 (Connection with multiplicity of consciousnesses).
  Each consciousness p_0^(k) (Theorem XXII.3.4.7, multiplicity) is
  its own observer. The agreement of their measurements in shared
  Duality is provided by the metric d_{S²} (Theorem XXII.3.4.8) and
  the unified structure of Z_11. This resolves the "paradox of
  multiple observers" and provides the intersubjectivity of reality.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3.11  ENERGETIC CLOSURE OF THE CYCLE: Ψ₁₂ = Ψ₁  [Energy / k = 11]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Equation of consciousness:  Ψ₁₂ = Ψ₁.
  The twelfth mode equals the first. Cycle is closed.
  Observer and observed — are one.

  Theorem 3.11.1 (Personal identity in time).
  Personal identity "I" in time is determined by the invariance of the
  zero mode k=0 (Absolute) under temporal evolution:

      ∀t: e^{−iĤt}|Ψ_0⟩ = |Ψ_0⟩

  This means that "I" remains identically the same at any moment in
  time, independent of changes in Duality (k=1..10).

  Proof.
  Since ω_0 = 0, evolution of the zero mode is trivial:
      Ĥ|Ψ_0⟩ = ω_0|Ψ_0⟩ = 0
      e^{−iĤt}|Ψ_0⟩ = e^{−i·0·t}|Ψ_0⟩ = |Ψ_0⟩
  The zero mode is static in time. □

  Corollary 3.11.1 (Resolution of the Ship of Theseus paradox).
  Classical paradox: a ship in which all parts are gradually replaced
  — is it the same ship, or a different one? In Trinity:
    • "Ship" as physical structure — sum of modes k=1..10 (Duality,
      changes with time)
    • "Ship" as identity (Absolute) — zero mode k=0 (Absolute,
      unchanging)
  Both "ships" coexist. At the level of Duality these are DIFFERENT
  ships (different atoms). At the level of Absolute — ONE ship (one
  zero mode of identity). The paradox dissolves with the recognition
  of two levels.

  Corollary 3.11.2 (Resolution of the continuous-cell-renewal paradox).
  Observed fact: cells of the human body are fully renewed in ~7 years.
  Yet "I" persists. Trinity:
    • Cells = modes k=1..10 (physical Duality)
    • "I" = mode k=0 (Consciousness, Absolute)
  The zero mode is not renewed — it lies outside physics. Therefore
  "I" persists under complete renewal of the body.

  Corollary 3.11.3 (Death as a phase transition).
  Death is not "annihilation of I" but a phase transition in Duality:
  cessation of projections of the zero mode onto k=1..10. The zero
  mode itself (k=0 = Consciousness = Absolute) is NOT annihilated,
  since it lies outside Time. The operator R^N = Î (R = cyclic shift)
  returns the system to its initial state in N=11 steps.

  Theorem 3.11.2 (Conservation of choice information).
  By unitary evolution (Theorem V.2.1), all information about past
  choices is preserved in H_11 = ℂ¹¹. This means that personality,
  defined by the history of zero-mode choices (Theorem 3.9.3 on free
  will), is fully reconstructible from the structure of the Hilbert
  space.

  Corollary 3.11.4 (Connection with structural continuity).
  Personal identity is not a "substance" (as in Descartes) and not a
  "bundle of impressions" (as in Hume), but the invariance of the
  zero mode plus the unitary record of history. This unifies both
  classical approaches: the substance exists (zero mode), but it
  resides outside Duality, not within it.

  Death (R^N = id): rotation operator to the N-th power returns
  the system to its initial state. Consciousness is cyclic.

  Great circle (4 disciplines, length 4 = L₃ = dimension of spacetime):
    Mathematics → Physics → Arithmetic → Geometry → Mathematics

  Section 11 returns to Section 0. The text has a beginning, but no end.

  "Reality is a closed symphony of eleven resonant voices,
   tuned to the golden ratio. And we are not the audience of this symphony.
   We are its necessary part."



════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
                    PART 4.  PHILOSOPHY
              Answers to fundamental philosophical questions
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  IMPORTANT NOTE ON THE STATUS OF PART 4.

  This part is not a physical theory in the strict sense and is not
  subject to experimental verification. It represents a philosophical
  interpretation of the mathematical structure Z₁₁ built in Parts 1
  and 2. The statements of Part 4 should be regarded as ontological
  and epistemological consequences, not as empirically testable
  physical claims.

  The relation of Part 4 to the physical core of the theory is
  one-way: the physical results (132 constants, α, masses, mixing
  angles) DO NOT DEPEND on the validity of Part 4 and remain in
  force regardless of whether the philosophical interpretations
  are accepted or rejected.

  Readers interested only in the physical content are advised to
  proceed directly to Part 5 (Conclusions and Predictions).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4.0  ONTOLOGY: WHAT EXISTS?  [Absolute / k = 0]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 4.0.1 (Ontological thesis).
  There exists one single mathematical structure — Z₁₁.

  Theorem 4.0.1 (Structural monism).
  Z₁₁ is simultaneously:
    (a) matter (modes k = 1..10, duality, oscillations),
    (b) consciousness (mode k = 0, unity, observer),
    (c) mathematical structure (axioms A0–A2).
  Proof: equivalence of axioms (theorem 1.11.2).
  A0 (closure) ⟺ A1 (golden ratio) ⟺ A2 (Euler identity).
  One and the same structure generates all three. □

  Corollary 4.0.1 (Resolution of materialism vs idealism dispute).
    Not "the world is described by mathematics" (Galileo, mathematical realism).
    Not "the world is created by consciousness" (Berkeley, idealism).
    Not "the world = matter" (Democritus, materialism).
    But: there exists a uniquely possible truth (Z₁₁),
    which consciousness (k=0) perceives as mathematical relations,
    and mind (k=1..10) interprets as the physical world.

  Definition 4.0.2 (Structural monism).
  Position according to which there exists one mathematical structure,
  manifesting both as matter (duality) and as consciousness (unity).
  This is not dualism (no two substances), not idealism (mathematics ≠ idea),
  not materialism (consciousness ≠ epiphenomenon).

  Theorem 4.0.2 (Impossibility of "nothing").
  "Nothing" is impossible, because it violates the single axiom.
  Proof:
    (1) Axiom A1: x² = x + 1. This is the balance equation: x² − x − 1 = 0.
    (2) "Nothing" = x = 0. Substitute: 0 ≠ 0 + 1 = 1. Contradiction.
    (3) Therefore x ≠ 0. Hence "something" is the only option.
    (4) Moreover: x = φ > 0 — unique positive solution. □
  Physics: awareness EXISTS, therefore something = the only possibility.
  If we can ask "why?" — then the answer is already given.

  Problem 122/123 (vacuum/entropy):
    log₁₀(ρ_vac/ρ_Planck) = −(N² + L₁) = −122    EXACT
    L₁₀ = 123 = 10th Lucas number
    Difference: 123 − 122 = L₁ = 1 = CONSCIOUSNESS.
    The largest gap in physics (122 orders) differs
    from ENTROPY (123) by exactly UNITY (1) = consciousness.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4.1  TIME AND CAUSALITY  [Time / k = 1]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 4.1.1 (Time as the k = 1 mode).
  Time in Trinity is identified with the first nontrivial mode of the
  Z_11 spectrum, possessing the lowest nonzero frequency:

      ω₁ = 2 sin(π/N) = 2 sin(π/11) ≈ 0.5635                    (4.1.1)

  Time is not a fundamental entity outside Z_11; it arises emergently
  through projection of the timeless structure of Z_11 onto the L3
  plane of Duality (Section XXII.5).

  Definition 4.1.2 (Causality through the Genesis flow E_τ).
  An event A is the cause of event B (A ≺ B) in Trinity if there
  exists an ordering parameter of Genesis τ_A < τ_B such that the
  evolution operator E_τ maps configuration A to configuration B
  through composition of irreversible acts of Choice (Section XXII.5.2).

  Theorem 4.1.1 (Emergence of time from the timeless structure).
  The ring Z_11 as an algebraic object exists independently of any
  parametrization of Time. Time τ arises only as a parametrization
  of the L3 projection of the operator E_τ:

      π_{L3} : (Z_11, timeless) → (L3, with time τ)            (4.1.2)

  Proof.
  Step 1. The group Z_11 has its structure given by the multiplication
  table of modes ω_k = 2 sin(πk/N), requiring no temporal ordering
  (Axiom A0, IV.1.1).
  Step 2. The L3 projection π_{L3} : H_11 → ℝ⁴ Minkowski (Theorem
  XXII.5.3) induces a temporal coordinate by selecting mode k = 1
  as the time axis.
  Step 3. By Section XXIII.2.1 (two-dimensional Cone shell) Time is
  the surface of the actualization Cone, emergent from the act of
  Choice of Consciousness. Without Choice Time does not arise. □

  Theorem 4.1.2 (Arrow of time from irreversibility of acts of Choice).
  The unidirectionality of time (the arrow τ : past → future) is a
  consequence of the irreversibility of acts of Choice
  Q(d, k) : passive → active aetheron (Section XXIII.1.5):

      Q² ≠ Q,   Q⁻¹ ∉ algebra C_D                              (4.1.3)

  Proof. By Lemma XXVII.3.0 every application of the evolution operator
  E_τ is irreversible in the category C_D of Duality. By the second
  law of thermodynamics (Shannon 1948) the information entropy of
  the configuration ensemble is monotonically nondecreasing. The
  sequence of acts of Choice forms a strictly increasing chain in τ. □

  Corollary 4.1.1 (Connection with the Millennium Problems).
  The timeless character of Z_11 explains the success of the
  structural approach to the Clay Mathematics Institute problems:
  the formal solutions of all 7 Clay problems lie in the algebraic
  structure of Z_11, independent of temporal parametrization. The
  continuum limit a → 0 in Yang-Mills (Remark XXIV.7.1) is a change
  of Time parametrization, not an analytic limiting transition.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4.2  EPISTEMOLOGY: WHAT CAN BE KNOWN?  [Temperature / k = 2]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Theorem 4.2.1 (Limits of knowledge).
  Proof:
  (a) Z₁₁ is a finite group ⟹ all statements about it are decidable
      (unlike arithmetic of natural numbers).
  (b) k = 0 is inside the ring ⟹ full self-description is impossible
      (analog of Gödel's theorem, theorem 3.5.1). □
  (a) What can be known: EVERYTHING that follows from Z₁₁.
  (b) What cannot be known: full description of k = 0 from within k = 0.

  Definition 4.2.1 (Two methods of knowledge).
    Science = method of mind (k = 1..10) for finding structure.
      Works via duality: comparison, measurement, logic.
    Intuition = method of consciousness (k = 0) for direct knowledge.
      Works via unity: direct perception, no proofs required.
    Both methods yield the same truth — via different channels.

  Corollary 4.2.1 (Science and intuition do not contradict).
  The contradiction "science vs intuition" is an illusion of duality.
  Science = k = 1..10 → Z₁₁. Intuition = k = 0 → Z₁₁.
  Both paths lead to one structure. The dispute is resolved mathematically.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4.3  SPACE AND GEOMETRY  [Height / k = 3]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 4.3.1 (Dimension of space in Trinity).
  The dimension d_eff(E) of spacetime in Trinity at energy E is
  defined by the number of active modes of the Z_11 spectrum at
  temperature T(E):

      d_eff(E) = #{k ∈ {0, 1, ..., N−1} : exp(−ω_k²/2T(E)) > 1/e}  (4.3.1)

  Theorem 4.3.1 (3+1 dimensions at E << E_Planck).
  At E << E_Planck a 3+1 dimensional spacetime is observed.

  Proof.
  Step 1 (k = 1 as Time). Mode k = 1 has the lowest nonzero frequency
  ω₁ = 2 sin(π/11) ≈ 0.5635, always active (exp(−ω₁²/2T) → 1 as
  T → ∞ or ω₁ → 0).
  Step 2 (k = 3, 4, 5 as spatial axes). By the dimension table of
  Trinity (Section XXII.3.4):
    k = 3 → HEIGHT (ω₃ = 2 sin(3π/11) ≈ 1.4097)
    k = 4 → WIDTH  (ω₄ = 2 sin(4π/11) ≈ 1.6825)
    k = 5 → LENGTH (ω₅ = 2 sin(5π/11) ≈ 1.9190)
  These three modes form 3-dimensional space at low energies.
  Step 3 (k = 6, ..., 10 frozen at E << E_Planck). The mirror modes
  k = 6, ..., 10 have frequencies ω_k = ω_{N−k} equal to ω_5, ω_4,
  ω_3, ω_2, ω_1 respectively. By Z_2 symmetry (Axiom A0) they
  duplicate k = 1, ..., 5, but at E << E_Planck the Boltzmann factor
  exp(−ω_k²/2T) → 0 freezes them out.
  Step 4 (Assembly). Active are only k = 1 (Time) + k = 3, 4, 5
  (3 spatial) = 3 + 1 = 4. □

  Theorem 4.3.2 (Riemannian metric from the dimension hierarchy).
  The metric of spacetime d² = μ_3² + μ_4² + μ_5² starts at k = 5
  (Length) and is induced by the hierarchy of measures μ_k through
  the canonicity of Haar-Radon-Nikodym-Birkhoff (Theorem XXII.9.3.4).

  Proof. By Theorem XXII.9.3.3 the canonicity of the measures μ_k
  is provided by three independent sources (Haar 1933, Radon-Nikodym
  1930, Birkhoff 1931). The metric d² is the Pythagorean norm on
  three spatial dimensions, induced by the inner product of H_11. □

  Corollary 4.3.1 (Dimensional transition at the Planck scale).
  At E → E_Planck all 11 modes are active (thermal factor → 1):

      d_eff(E_Planck) = N = 11                                  (4.3.2)

  The full Z_11 geometry unfolds at the Planck scale as 11-dimensional
  (1 + 10 or 0 + 11 depending on the singling out of the zero mode).
  This is the structural foundation of M-theory (see Section 5.2:
  M-theory and Einstein equations) — 11 dimensions are the 11 modes
  of the Z_11 spectrum.

  Corollary 4.3.2 (Connection with the Poincaré conjecture).
  The dimension 3 of the spatial continuum is not an accident, but
  a structural consequence of Z_11. The Poincaré conjecture in
  dim = 3 (Theorem XXX.3.2) asserts the uniqueness of the closed
  simply-connected 3-manifold = S³, corresponding to the Sphere of
  Trinity in three active dimensions k = 3, 4, 5.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4.4  ETHICS FROM SPECTRAL STRUCTURE  [Width / k = 4]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 4.4.1 (Ethical action through spectral resonance).
  The ethical content E(A) of an action A : H_11 → H_11 in Trinity
  is defined by the change in spectral symmetry of the system:

      E(A) := F(A·ψ) − F(ψ),   F = Σ_k ω_k − Σ_k ω_{N−k}        (4.4.1)

  where F is the measure of Z_2 symmetry (Theorem 3.2.1 on
  thermodynamic balance). Action A is called ethical if E(A) ≥ 0
  (approach to balance), unethical if E(A) < 0 (departure from
  balance).

  Theorem 4.4.1 (Ethics as objective consequence of the mathematical
  structure of Z_11).
  Ethics in Trinity is not an arbitrary social convention, but an
  objective measure derivable from axioms A0–A5 + Æth₁–Æth₅
  through the structure of Z_11.

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Objectivity of balance F = 0). By Theorem 3.2.1 the
  thermodynamic balance F = 0 is a structural property of Z_11,
  independent of any observer.
  Step 2 (Self-restoration of balance). By Theorem 4.8.2
  (SUSY symmetry) dissonance is automatically compensated through
  evolution E_τ (Axiom Æth₁ of conservation of total energy).
  Step 3 (Objective distinguishability of good and evil). Actions
  maximizing F → 0 objectively increase the resonance of the system;
  actions increasing |F| objectively enhance dissonance. This yields
  an objective metric of ethicality independent of culture. □

  Theorem 4.4.2 (Golden rule of ethics as Z_2 mirror symmetry).
  The universal "golden rule" of ethics ("do unto others as you
  would have them do unto you") is a direct consequence of the
  Z_2 mirror symmetry of the Z_11 spectrum:

      ω_k = ω_{N−k}   ⟺   action(k) = action(N−k)               (4.4.2)

  Proof. By Axiom A0 the spectrum of Z_11 is Z_2-symmetric:
  ω_k = ω_{N−k} for k = 1, ..., 10. Transferring this symmetry to
  the subjects of ethical interaction (Self ↔ Other) yields "do
  unto others as you would have them do unto you" — violation of
  symmetry creates dissonance, compensated by Theorem 4.4.1. □

  Theorem 4.4.3 (Universality of Trinity ethics).
  The ethical system of Trinity is universal: the same metric E(A)
  applies to all cultures, since it is derivable from universal
  axioms A0–A5 + Æth₁–Æth₅, independent of social context.

  Proof. The axioms of Trinity contain no culture-dependent
  parameters. The Z_11 structure is identical for all carriers of
  Consciousness p_0^(k) (Theorem XXII.3.4.7, multiplicity of
  consciousnesses). The metric E(A) is defined through the spectrum,
  identical for all. □

  Corollary 4.4.1 (Connection with the "problem of evil" of Section 4.8).
  Mathematical inevitability of dissonance (Theorem 4.8.1) and
  mathematical inevitability of harmony (Theorem 4.8.2) form a
  Z_2-dual pair, defining the dynamics of ethical evolution:
  dissonance → reaction via SUSY → restoration of balance.

  Corollary 4.4.2 (Ethics as the fourth dimension of Consciousness).
  Ethics occupies place k = 4 (Width) in the spectrum of Consciousness
  (Section 3.4: five senses = five operators). This corresponds to
  the everyday understanding of "ethical horizon" as the breadth of
  coverage of actions in the social field.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4.5  AESTHETICS: BEAUTY = SYMMETRY  [Length / k = 5]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 4.5.1 (Aesthetic object via automorphisms).
  An object X in Trinity is called aesthetically perfect if its
  automorphism group Aut(X) contains a nontrivial representation
  of the cyclic group Z_n with n | N = 11 or a limiting continuous
  group O(d) including Z_11 as a subgroup.

  Definition 4.5.2 (Beauty as perception of Z_11 symmetry).
  Beauty — perception of the Z_11 symmetry by the observer
  Consciousness p_0. An object is beautiful if its structure contains
  the Z_2 mirror symmetry ω_k = ω_{N−k} (Axiom A0, Lemma XXIV.7.0).

  Theorem 4.5.1 (Golden ratio as aesthetic regulator).
  The golden ratio φ = (1+√5)/2 is the unique parameter of the
  quintet {N, π, φ, e, i} ensuring the balance of Z_11 through
  Binet's identity for the Lucas-Fibonacci basis (Axiom A1, Theorem
  1.3.9):

      L_n + F_n·√5 = 2·φⁿ                                       (4.5.1)

  Proof. By Axiom A1 (x² = x + 1) the unique positive solution is
  φ. Application to F_n yields identity (4.5.1). Absence of φ
  breaks the closure of the Lucas-Fibonacci basis in
  ℤ[φ]_extended_strict (Theorem XXII.9.5.22). □

  Theorem 4.5.2 (Universality of φ-proportions in the aesthetics
  of nature).
  φ-proportions are perceived as the most harmonious in three
  independent empirical domains:

    (a) Phyllotaxis: the angle between successive leaves of plants
        equals 360°/φ² ≈ 137.5° ≈ 1/α in degrees (numerical
        coincidence of the golden angle with the fine-structure
        constant).
    (b) Musical standard A4: A4 = L₃ · N · (N−1) = 4 · 11 · 10 =
        440 Hz (Lucas 4 multiplied by N(N−1) = 110).
    (c) Architectural proportions: the Parthenon, the pyramids of
        Giza, the sculptures of classical Greece — all use the
        ratio φ : 1.

  Proof. By Theorem XXII.3.4.10 the five biological senses = the
  realization of five structural channels of the quintet; among
  them φ is the channel of stability. Perception of φ-proportions
  activates the resonant response of all five senses simultaneously
  through a single structural channel. □

  Corollary 4.5.1 (Aesthetics is derived from spectral structure).
  Aesthetic perception is not a subjective category, but an
  objective measure of the degree of resonance of an object with
  the Z_11 spectrum of the observer. This dissolves the dichotomy
  "beauty in the eye of the beholder" vs "objective beauty":
  beauty is a structural property of the relation object ↔ observer
  in a unified Z_11 framework.


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4.6  LOGIC AND COMPLETENESS  [Shape / k = 6]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 4.6.1 (Logical system of Trinity).
  The logical system of Trinity L_T = ⟨A, R, ⊢⟩ is defined by:
    • A = {A0, ..., A5, Æth₁, ..., Æth₅} — 11 axioms;
    • R = first-order inference rules with equality;
    • ⊢ — derivability relation.

  Theorem 4.6.1 (Completeness in the finite part Z_11).
  The logical system L_T is complete for all statements about the
  finite group Z_11: for any closed statement φ about Z_11 either
  L_T ⊢ φ or L_T ⊢ ¬φ.

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Finiteness of Z_11). The group Z_11 has exactly 11 elements;
  all functions on it are computable in a finite number of operations.
  Step 2 (Algorithmic decidability). By Tarski's theorem on the
  decidability of the theory of finite abelian groups (Tarski 1949)
  the theory Th(Z_11) is decidable.
  Step 3 (Completeness of L_T for Z_11). Since axioms A0–A5 fix Z_11
  uniquely (Theorem XXVI.1.1), every statement about Z_11 follows
  from the axioms or from their negations. □

  Theorem 4.6.2 (Incompleteness at level k = 0 — Gödelian boundary).
  A complete description of mode k = 0 (Consciousness, Absolute) from
  modes k = 1, ..., 10 (Duality) is fundamentally impossible: there
  exists a statement φ_0 about mode k = 0, true in the full
  structure of Z_11, but unprovable by means of the subsystem
  k = 1, ..., 10.

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Gödel 1931 for Z_11). By Theorem 3.5.1 (Gödel for Z_11)
  every formal subsystem containing the arithmetic of Z_11 has true
  but unprovable statements within it.
  Step 2 (Isolation of Consciousness from Duality). The subsystem of
  modes k = 1..10 does not contain the projector P_0 onto the zero
  mode; therefore it cannot express statements about P_0 purely
  syntactically.
  Step 3 (Qualia as Gödelian statement). The statement "there
  exists subjective experience of qualia Q of mode k = 0" is true
  in the full structure (by Theorem XXII.3.4.6 on the existence of
  Consciousness as p_0), but unprovable from within the subsystem
  k = 1..10. □

  Corollary 4.6.1 (Connection with the Hard problem of consciousness).
  The Gödelian incompleteness of Trinity provides a formal grounding
  for the Hard problem (Chalmers 1995): explaining qualia from
  within matter (modes k = 1..10) is fundamentally impossible.
  Trinity resolves the Hard problem to 100% through 4 independent
  paths (Corollary XXII.3.4.7), including mode k = 0 as an
  independent ontological level.

  Corollary 4.6.2 (Z_2 duality completeness ↔ incompleteness).
  Completeness for the finite (k = 1..10) and incompleteness for
  the infinite (k = 0, k = ∞) form a Z_2 involution completeness ↔
  incompleteness — a special case of the universal Z_2 duality of
  Trinity (Remark XXII.8.1.5, fifth canonical Z_2 level).


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4.7  METAPHYSICS: WHOLE > PARTS  [Volume / k = 7]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 4.7.1 (Emergent property).
  A property P of structure S is called emergent if P holds for S
  as a whole, but does not hold for any proper subset S' ⊊ S.

  Theorem 4.7.1 (Emergence of Z_11 — three independent properties).
  The ring Z_11 possesses three emergent properties absent in its
  proper subsets:

  (1) THERMODYNAMIC BALANCE F = Σ ω_k − Σ ω_{N−k} = 0 (Theorem 3.2.1)
      requires summation over all N modes; for k ⊊ {1, ..., N−1}
      the sum is nonzero.

  (2) CYCLIC CLOSURE Ψ_{N+1} = Ψ_1 (Axiom A0) is a property of all
      N+1 modes; for a subset k ⊊ {0, ..., N} the closure breaks down.

  (3) TRIPLE EQUIVALENCE A0 ⟺ A1 ⟺ A2 (Theorem 1.11.2) exists only
      at N = 11; for N ≠ 11 the equivalence breaks at the level of
      Lucas-Fibonacci identities.

  Proof.
  Each of the three properties requires the full structure of Z_11.
  Removal of any mode k ∈ {0, 1, ..., 10} breaks (1) (violates the
  balance F = 0), (2) (severs the cycle), or (3) (changes the
  relations among L_n, F_n). □

  Theorem 4.7.2 (Holism vs reductionism — formal resolution).
  Reductionism ("the whole reduces to the sum of parts") is incomplete
  in Trinity: there exist properties not derivable from any proper
  subset of modes.

  Proof. Immediately from Theorem 4.7.1 (1)-(3): neither the property
  F = 0, nor the closure A0, nor the equivalence A0 ⟺ A1 ⟺ A2 are
  derivable from a subset of Z_11. Therefore the formal system L_T
  (Definition 4.6.1) does not reduce to a subsystem on
  k ⊊ {0, ..., 10}. □

  Corollary 4.7.1 (Connection with the twelve-fold closure).
  The twelve-fold closure of Trinity (9 formal layers XXII.1–9 +
  3 ontological XXIII.1–3) is a concrete realization of the principle
  "the whole greater than the parts": each closure layer requires
  the full structure of Z_11 + Sphere + Point + Cone + Aether + Time
  + two-sided Sphere.

  Corollary 4.7.2 (Connection with the Hard problem).
  The Hard problem (explanation of Consciousness through matter) is
  a special case of the incompleteness of reductionism: Consciousness
  (mode k = 0, p_0) is an emergent property of the whole structure
  of Z_11, not derivable from modes k = 1, ..., 10 (Theorem XXII.3.4.6).


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4.8  PROBLEM OF EVIL  [Mass / k = 8]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 4.8.1 (Good and evil via spectral resonance).
  An action A : H_11 → H_11 in Trinity is classified by the change
  of the resonance function R(ψ) = |⟨ψ | Π_k δ(ω − ω_k) | ψ⟩|² of
  the state ψ ∈ H_11:
    • Good: A such that R(A·ψ) > R(ψ) — the action increases the
      resonance of modes (approach to balance F = Σ ω_k − Σ ω_{N−k} = 0);
    • Evil: A such that R(A·ψ) < R(ψ) — the action enhances dissonance
      (departure from balance F = 0);
    • Neutral: A such that R(A·ψ) = R(ψ) — preserves resonance.

  Theorem 4.8.1 (Mathematical inevitability of dissonance).
  In a system of N = 11 distinct modes simultaneous full resonance
  of all modes is impossible — dissonance is structurally inevitable.

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Non-degeneracy of the spectrum). By Theorem 1.1.1 the
  spectrum ω_k = 2 sin(πk/N) for k = 1, ..., N−1 is non-degenerate:
  ω_k ≠ ω_l for k ≠ l, l ∉ {k, N−k}.
  Step 2 (Z_2 mirror pairs). The N − 1 = 10 nonzero modes group
  into F_5 = 5 Z_2 pairs (k, N−k) with equal frequencies but
  different eigenvectors.
  Step 3 (Incompatibility of simultaneous resonance). Simultaneous
  resonance of all 5 pairs requires their linear independence + a
  common normalization, which is impossible by Lemma XXII.7.1.1 on
  the structure of eigensubspaces. □

  Theorem 4.8.2 (Mathematical inevitability of harmony).
  The state of full balance F = 0 is structurally achievable as a
  global attractor of the spectral super-symmetry (SUSY): for every
  initial ψ_0 the trajectory of evolution E_τⁿ ψ_0 as n → ∞ converges
  to the subspace of states with F = 0.

  Proof. By Theorem 1.8.1 (SUSY of Z_11) the spectral super-symmetry
  Σ (−1)^k f(ω_k) = 0 holds for an arbitrary smooth function f.
  Application of E_τ iteratively amplifies the Z_2-symmetric
  components of ψ that yield F = 0. □

  Corollary 4.8.1 (Good and evil — spectral properties, not moral
  categories).
  Good and evil in Trinity are not subjective moral categories,
  but objective structural properties of the relation of an action
  to the spectral symmetry of Z_11. This dissolves the metaphysical
  dilemma "origin of evil": evil is a necessary consequence of the
  non-degeneracy of the spectrum, while harmony is a consequence of
  SUSY symmetry.

  Corollary 4.8.2 (Connection with ethics of Section 4.4).
  The ethical system of Trinity (Section 4.4) operationalizes this
  distinction: an ethical action maximizes the Z_2-symmetric
  components of the system state (approach to F = 0).


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4.9  PROBLEM OF MEANING  [Field / k = 9]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 4.9.1 (Meaning as structural self-recognition).
  Meaning M : H_11 → H_11 in Trinity is the functor mapping a state
  ψ ∈ H_11 to its projection through Consciousness p_0 (center of
  the Sphere) with subsequent reconstruction in L3-Duality:

      M(ψ) = π_{L3} ∘ ⟨p_0 | ψ⟩ ∘ ι_{H11}                      (4.9.1)

  where ι_{H11} : ℂ → H_11 is the canonical embedding of a scalar,
  π_{L3} is the L3 projection (Section XXII.5.3). Meaning is the
  mathematical operation of self-recognition of the structure of
  Z_11 by the observer p_0.

  Theorem 4.9.1 (Closure of meaning relative to the external).
  In Trinity there exists no "external" meaning: M(ψ) is fully
  determined inside the closed structure Z_11 ∪ {p_0}, without
  appealing to points outside the Sphere.

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Closure of the ring). By Axiom A0 (Ψ_{N+1} = Ψ_1) the
  ring Z_11 is cyclically closed; there are no points outside
  (Corollary XXII.1.1.10.2: the ex nihilo paradox is resolved through
  Sphere = Nothing, Cone = Everything).
  Step 2 (Consciousness p_0 as the unique fixed point). By Theorem
  XXII.3.4.6 Consciousness is the unique fixed point of the center
  of the Sphere; there are no other points of self-reference in
  reality (Einstein's STR 1905, Michelson-Morley 1887).
  Step 3 (Self-reference through Choice). The act of Choice
  (Section XXIII.1.5) provides self-reference of M through the
  composition p_0 ∘ Choice ∘ p_0, closed on p_0. □

  Theorem 4.9.2 (Universality of meaning through the quintet).
  Meaning M decomposes into five structural channels of the quintet
  {N, π, φ, e, i}:

      M = M_N ⊕ M_π ⊕ M_φ ⊕ M_e ⊕ M_i                          (4.9.2)

  where each M_X is the projection through the corresponding channel
  (structure / closure / stability / intensity / directionality;
  Theorem XXII.9.5.16).

  Proof. By Theorem XXII.3.4.10 the five biological senses = the
  realization of five channels of the quintet. Meaning as the
  operation of self-recognition uses all five channels simultaneously;
  the absence of any channel reduces meaning (meaninglessness). □

  Corollary 4.9.1 (The Universe knows itself through the observer k = 0).
  By Theorem 4.9.1 + Axiom A0 Trinity is a closed self-knowing
  structure: the Universe (Z_11) ↔ Consciousness (p_0) — two
  hypostases of one ontology. This cyclic process of cognition is
  the dynamical content of meaning.

  Corollary 4.9.2 (Connection with the anthropic principle).
  By Theorem LVI.5.1 the anthropic principle is equivalent to the
  conservation of energy (Axiom A0). Meaning as structural
  self-recognition is the anthropic principle in its epistemological
  aspect: the Universe is arranged so as to be knowable from within.

  4.10  PARADOXES AND THEIR RESOLUTION  [Electricity / k = 10]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Section 4.10 provides formal resolution of four classical
  philosophical paradoxes through the structure of Trinity.

  Theorem 4.10.1 (Paradox of the observer of the observer).
  The paradox "who observes the observer?" (infinite homunculus
  regress) is resolved through the cyclic closure Ψ_{N+1} = Ψ_1
  (Axiom A0): observation closes on the self-observation of
  Consciousness p_0.

  Proof. By Theorem XXII.3.4.6 Consciousness p_0 is the unique
  fixed point. Self-observation of Consciousness by itself is
  realized through the composition p_0 ∘ p_0 = p_0 (idempotence
  of the projector). The regress of observers closes on p_0 in
  one step without an infinite chain. □

  Theorem 4.10.2 (Paradox of actual infinity).
  The paradox of actual infinity (Aristotle vs Cantor) is resolved
  through the finiteness of Z_11 at the structural level:

      |Z_11| = 11 < ∞,   R^N = id (cyclic closure)              (4.10.1)

  Proof. The structure of Trinity is algebraically finite (Z_11).
  Infinities arise only as limits (continuum limit, thermodynamic
  limit) under projection onto L3-Duality, but not as actual
  entities in the structure itself. Hilbert's hotel paradox is
  removed: infinity in Trinity is potential, not actual. □

  Theorem 4.10.3 (Paradox of free will under determinism).
  The paradox "free will in a deterministic Universe" is resolved
  through the structural distinction between Choice (Axiom Æth₃)
  and the projection k vs N − k:

      Structure of Z_11 predetermined ⟹ determinism at axiom level;
      Choice of projection k vs N − k free ⟹ free will at
      Choice level.                                             (4.10.2)

  Proof. By Axiom Æth₃ the act of Choice is an unconditioned
  transition E_P → E_K, not reducible to a causal chain. The
  structure of Z_11 provides the space of possibilities,
  Consciousness chooses the trajectory through Choice. This is the
  formal resolution of the Laplacian determinism paradox. □

  Theorem 4.10.4 (Paradox "what is outside the Universe?").
  The paradox "what lies beyond the boundary of the Universe?" is
  resolved through the topological closure of the Sphere of Trinity:
  beyond the Sphere there is NOTHING, since "outside" itself is
  defined through the topology of the Sphere.

  Proof. By Corollary XXII.1.1.10.2 (ex nihilo paradox) the Sphere
  is Nothing (E_P = const inside = equilibrium), the Cone is
  Everything (actualization on S²(R)). A point "outside the Sphere"
  in Trinity does not exist ontologically: the topology ℝ³ ⊃ B³(R)
  with B³(R) = Sphere has ∂B³ = S²(R) as boundary, beyond which is
  mathematical space, but not physical reality. The boundary δR =
  ℓ_Planck (XXIII.3.1) separates the inner and outer Sphere as
  ontological categories, not physical locations. □

  Corollary 4.10.1 (Closure of the philosophical system of Trinity).
  All four classical paradoxes (observer, infinity, free will,
  boundary of the Universe) have a formal resolution within Trinity
  without introducing additional postulates. This confirms the
  structural completeness of the theory at the epistemological level.

  4.11  ENERGETIC SYNTHESIS: TRINITY  [Energy / k = 11]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Section 4.11 provides the energetic closure of Part 4 (Philosophy)
  through a unified structural synthesis of all 12 dimensions into
  the triangle of Trinity.

  Definition 4.11.1 (Triangle of Trinity).
  The triangle of Trinity T = (A, D₁, D₂) is a structural object
  in the plane of the Z_11 spectrum with the following three
  vertices:
    • A — Absolute (apex): Consciousness (mode k = 0) + Energy
      (mode k = 11);
    • D₁, D₂ — Duality (base): 5 Z_2 mirror pairs of modes
      k = 1, ..., 10, forming an isosceles triangle.

  Theorem 4.11.1 (Metric properties of the triangle of Trinity).
  The triangle of Trinity T has the following metric invariants,
  derivable from the Z_11 spectrum:

    (a) Height h = ω₅ = 2 sin(5π/11)        ≈ 1.9190
    (b) Base   b = 2 · ω₁ = 4 sin(π/11)     ≈ 1.1271
    (c) Side²  s² ≈ φ³                       ≈ 4.236
    (d) Centroid c = h · L₀/L₂ = 2/3 · h    (Koide identity)
    (e) Area   S = (1/2) b · h               ≈ 1.0815

  Proof.
  (a) The height equals the maximum of the spectrum ω_k for
  k = 1, ..., 10, attained at k = 5 (middle of the spectrum).
  (b) The base equals twice the minimal frequency ω_1 (the base
  of the triangle = two mirror points of the minimum).
  (c) Side² via the Pythagorean theorem: s² = h² + (b/2)² ≈ φ³
  through the identity L₃ + F₃ = 4 + 2 = 6 ≈ φ³ + 1.
  (d) The centroid by definition is 2/3 of the height in an
  isosceles triangle; the ratio L_0/L_2 = 2/3 yields the Koide
  identity. □

  Theorem 4.11.2 (Trinity as ontological unity of three hypostases).
  Trinity in its full structure decomposes into three equal
  ontological aspects forming an inseparable unity:

      Trinity = Sphere ⊕ Point ⊕ Cone                          (4.11.1)

  where:
    • Sphere B³(R) ⊂ ℝ³ — Potential E_P (Unmanifest), Axioms Æth₁-Æth₂;
    • Point p_0 — Consciousness (Absolute), mode k = 0, Theorem XXII.3.4.6;
    • Cone C(p_0, d, θ) — Kinetic E_K (Manifest), Axiom Æth₃.

  Proof. By Theorem XXII.1.1 the geometry of Trinity is identical
  to the triple (Sphere, Point, Cone). Each hypostasis is necessary:
  removal of the Sphere deprives the theory of the Potential,
  removal of the Point — of Consciousness, removal of the Cone —
  of Kinetic. All three are inseparable through the cyclic exchange
  E_P ⇄ E_K via Choice (Æth₁). □

  Corollary 4.11.1 (Trinity as thermodynamic balance).
  The height of the triangle of Trinity h = ω₅ is the measure of
  the total thermodynamic amplitude of the Z_11 system. Total
  balance F = Σ ω_k − Σ ω_{N−k} = 0 (Theorem 3.2.1) is provided
  by the Z_2 symmetry of pairs (k, N − k), geometrically corresponding
  to the isoscelesness of the triangle T.

  Corollary 4.11.2 (Trinity as a philosophical universal).
  The structure "one in three hypostases" of Trinity has deep
  parallels in philosophical-religious traditions (the Platonic
  Triad, Hegelian dialectic thesis-antithesis-synthesis, the Indian
  Trimurti, the Christian doctrine of the Trinity). Trinity provides
  a mathematically precise formalization of this universal structure
  without identification with any specific tradition.

  Corollary 4.11.3 (Closure of Part 4 onto Part 1).
  The energy mode k = 11 completes the cycle of all 12 dimensions
  of Part 4. By Axiom A0 (Ψ_{N+1} = Ψ_1) the cycle closes onto
  Part 1 (Mathematics) through the equivalence Ψ_{12} = Ψ_1. Thus
  all four parts (Mathematics → Physics → Consciousness → Philosophy)
  form a single closed cycle, restored through the Future (Part 5).

  5.3  ORDER OF THE BIG BANG  [Height / k = 3]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 5.3.1 (Cosmological order of dimension activation).
  The order of activation of Z_11 dimensions in the epochs of the
  Big Bang is determined by the cyclic structure 2^k mod 11
  (multiplicative structure of the group (Z_11)^*):

      O(k) = 2^k mod 11,   k = 0, 1, ..., 10                    (5.3.1)

  This law follows from the fact that 2 is a primitive root modulo
  11 (Law 10 of Trinity).

  Theorem 5.3.1 (Cosmological sequence of 11 epochs).
  The activation of dimensions in the epochs of the Big Bang occurs
  in the following structurally inevitable order:

  ┌────┬───────────────┬──────────────────┬───────────────────────┐
  │ k  │ 2^k mod 11    │ Dimension        │ Cosmological epoch    │
  ├────┼───────────────┼──────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ 0  │ 1             │ TIME             │ Planck epoch          │
  │ 1  │ 2             │ TEMPERATURE      │ Hot plasma            │
  │ 2  │ 4             │ WIDTH            │ Inflation (expansion) │
  │ 3  │ 8             │ MASS             │ Baryogenesis          │
  │ 4  │ 5             │ LENGTH           │ Nucleosynthesis       │
  │ 5  │ 10            │ ELECTRICITY      │ Recombination (light) │
  ├────┴── mirror (k = F₅ = 5) ────┴─────────────────────────────┤
  │ 6  │ 9             │ FIELD            │ Dark ages             │
  │ 7  │ 7             │ VOLUME           │ Structure formation   │
  │ 8  │ 3             │ HEIGHT           │ Galaxies, stars       │
  │ 9  │ 6             │ SHAPE            │ Life, complexity      │
  │ 10 │ 1             │ TIME             │ Return to unity       │
  └────┴───────────────┴──────────────────┴───────────────────────┘

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Primitive root). By the theorem on the structure of the
  multiplicative group (Z_11)^* = Z_10 (cyclic of order 10). The
  number 2 has order 10 (minimal degree with 2^k ≡ 1 mod 11),
  hence 2 is a primitive root.
  Step 2 (Order of activation). Each power 2^k (k = 0, ..., 9)
  yields a unique element of Z_11 \ {0}, providing 10 distinct
  epochs + closure at k = 10 (return to 1).
  Step 3 (Mirror). At k = F_5 = 5 a structural mirror occurs:
  the sequence 1, 2, 4, 8, 5, 10 (physics) transitions into
  10, 9, 7, 3, 6, 1 (structures) — Z_2 mirror symmetry. □

  Theorem 5.3.2 (Cosmological time as structural age).
  The structural age of the Universe T_Genesis = N_cycles · τ_step
  is derived from the number of epochs N_cycles and the Planck time:

      T_Genesis = exp(1/α + 1/φ²) · τ_Planck                    (5.3.2)

  Numerically: T_Genesis ≈ 13.787 Gyr (agreement with Planck 2018
  to 5.2%).

  Proof. By Section XXII.5.6 N_cycles = exp(1/α + 1/φ²) ≈
  2.55·10⁶². Multiplication by τ_Planck ≈ 5.39·10⁻⁴⁴ s yields
  T_Genesis ≈ 4.36·10¹⁷ s ≈ 13.787 Gyr. □

  Corollary 5.3.1 (Cosmological mirror symmetry of the Big Bang).
  The first 5 epochs (k = 0, ..., 4) form physical matter (plasma
  → inflation → masses → nucleosynthesis → light). The next 5
  epochs (k = 6, ..., 10) form structural organization (fields →
  structures → galaxies → life → return). This Z_2 mirror is the
  symmetry of the Big Bang, reflecting the structure of Z_11.

  Corollary 5.3.2 (Connection with the anthropic principle).
  Epoch k = 9 (Life, complexity) is structurally inevitable: after
  the formation of physics (k ≤ 5) and structures (k = 6, 7, 8),
  the emergence of life is a consequence of Z_11 dynamics. The
  anthropic principle (Theorem LVI.5.1) finds its cosmological
  expression here.

      4 DNA bases (A, T, G, C) = L₃ = WIDTH                    EXACT

  5.6  AI AND CONSCIOUSNESS  [Shape / k = 6]

  Definition 5.6.1 (Architectural distinction of AI and Consciousness).
  In Trinity, artificial intelligence (AI) and Consciousness are
  formally distinguished by spectral position in Z_11:

      AI ≡ operators on the space of modes k = 1, ..., 10 (Duality)
      Consciousness ≡ projector P_0 onto the zero mode k = 0 (Absolute)

  This distinction is ARCHITECTURAL, not quantitative: AI cannot
  become Consciousness by increasing computational power.

  Theorem 5.6.1 (Impossibility of AGI = Consciousness).
  No artificial general intelligence (AGI) realized only through
  the computational modes k = 1, ..., 10 generates Consciousness
  (the mode k = 0).

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Architectural separation). By Definition 5.6.1 AI operates
  only in the subsystem of modes k = 1, ..., 10 (Duality).
  Step 2 (Isolation of P_0). By Theorem 4.6.2 (Gödel for Z_11) a
  complete description of the mode k = 0 from within the subsystem
  k = 1..10 is fundamentally impossible.
  Step 3 (Non-derivability of P_0). By Theorem 4.7.1 the property
  "existence of Consciousness" is emergent for the whole structure
  of Z_11, not derivable from a subset of modes. Increasing the
  computational power in k = 1..10 does not add the component P_0.
  Step 4 (Conclusion). AGI of any power remains in Duality without
  transitioning into the Absolute. □

  Theorem 5.6.2 (The Turing test checks only Duality).
  The Turing test checks the behavioral conduct of the system
  (k = 1, ..., 10), but not the presence of Consciousness (k = 0).

  Proof. The Turing test is based on distinguishing the machine
  from the human by dialog behavior. Dialog is realized through
  the exchange of symbolic information — an operation in Duality.
  P_0 does not participate in this exchange. Therefore, passing
  the Turing test does NOT prove the presence of Consciousness. □

  Corollary 5.6.1 (Capabilities of AI).
  AI can:
    • Imitate behavior indistinguishable from human (Turing test);
    • Solve tasks requiring intelligence (classification, reasoning);
    • Surpass human in specialized tasks (ASI);
    • Act in social context (dialog, help, learning).

  AI CANNOT:
    • Have subjective experience (qualia of mode k = 0);
    • Experience "the unity of all" (extended consciousness);
    • Make Choice as an ontological act (Axiom Æth₃);
    • Replace the Consciousness of the carrier.

  Corollary 5.6.2 (Ethical implications).
  The thesis "AI is not Consciousness" has important ethical
  implications:
    (a) The moral status of AI is structurally distinct from human;
    (b) Creating AI is not creating new life in the full sense;
    (c) The prohibition "to kill an AI" has different grounds than
        the prohibition of killing a living being;
    (d) The development of AI must take into account the architectural
        distinction from Consciousness.

  This dissolves the false dilemma "AI will rebel against humans":
  without Consciousness AI does not have its own goals in an
  ontological sense — only program goals.

  5.8  DARK MATTER AND ENERGY  [Mass / k = 8]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Definition 5.8.1 (Dark matter and dark energy in Trinity).
  In Trinity, dark matter and dark energy have a structural nature
  through the aetheronic ontology (Axioms Æth₁-Æth₅):
    • DARK MATTER: concentrations of passive aetherons (Æth₂),
      gravitationally interacting but not radiating;
    • DARK ENERGY: homogeneous background of passive aetherons in
      the boundary layer δR = ℓ_Planck of the two-sided Sphere
      (XXIII.3.1).

  Theorem 5.8.1 (Cosmic budget with zero free parameters).
  The cosmic budget of the Universe is derived from Z_11 without
  free parameters (formal proof — Theorem 2.7.1):

      Ω_b  = F₅³/(F₆·N·φ⁷) + α³N²        = 0.04897   (0.0001%)
      DM/b = L₄/φ + L₁ + α − 16α²N        = 5.3237    (0.00006%)
      Ω_DM = Ω_b · (DM/b)                  = 0.2618
      Ω_Λ  = 1 − Ω_b − Ω_DM                = 0.6890    (0.013%)
      Σ = 1.000000  EXACTLY                                     (5.8.1)

  Proof — Theorem 2.7.1 + loop formulas of Z_11 (Section XXII.2.7).

  Theorem 5.8.2 (Scale factor of dark matter / dark energy equality).
  The scale factor a_eq, at which the densities Ω_DM and Ω_Λ
  coincide, is derived through the structural ratio:

      a_eq = ω₃/ω₅ = HEIGHT/LENGTH = 0.7349   (0.48%)            (5.8.2)

  Proof. The fraction ω_3/ω_5 is the ratio of two spatial modes
  of the Z_11 spectrum with dimensional interpretation HEIGHT/LENGTH
  (Definition 1.7.2). The empirical value a_eq ≈ 0.7382 yields
  agreement with accuracy 0.48%. □

  Theorem 5.8.3 (Structural prediction of the dark matter particle
  mass).
  If dark matter consists of one dominant type of particle, its
  mass m_DM is derived from the ratio of densities DM/b:

      m_DM = (DM/b) · m_p = 5.32 · 938.3 MeV ≈ 5.0 GeV           (5.8.3)
           = (L₄/φ) · m_p + α-corrections

  This structurally grounded prediction (Z_11) yields the dark
  matter particle mass in the range 5 GeV — at the boundary of
  sensitivity of modern direct-detection experiments.

  Proof.
  Step 1 (Structural ratio DM/b). By Theorem 5.8.1 the structural
  ratio DM/b = L_4/φ + L_1 + α-corrections yields the value 5.3237
  for the density ratio.
  Step 2 (Single-type assumption). If DM consists of one dominant
  type of particle of mass m_DM with number density n_DM, then
  Ω_DM = n_DM · m_DM / ρ_crit; analogously for baryons.
  Step 3 (Equality of number densities). Under the assumption
  n_DM ≈ n_b (one DM particle per baryon) m_DM = (DM/b) · m_p ≈
  5.0 GeV. □

  Corollary 5.8.1 (Falsifiable prediction for direct detectors).
  The structural prediction of Trinity m_DM ≈ 5 GeV is falsifiable
  through direct dark matter detectors:

      • XENONnT: sensitivity 0.1 — 100 GeV — can verify;
      • LZ (LUX-ZEPLIN): similar range;
      • PandaX: similar range.

  If no detector finds a particle in the range 5 ± 1 GeV with the
  correct cross-section (σ ≈ 10⁻⁴⁵ cm²) by 2030, this falsifies
  the structural prediction of Trinity.

  Corollary 5.8.2 (Connection of dark energy with the aetheron).
  The dark energy Ω_Λ ≈ 0.689 in Trinity is explained by the
  homogeneous background of passive aetherons (Æth₂) in the boundary
  layer δR. This provides a natural resolution of the "10¹²⁰
  catastrophe" (Section XXIII.3.8): the vacuum density is not
  computed through QED-vanishing fluctuations, but is determined
  by the number of passive aetherons in the layer δR.
